name: Meter Intent
summary: A bounded-scalar readout — a static quantitative value within a known range (disk usage, score, capacity, password strength). Adopts the native `<meter>` / `role=meter` vocabulary verbatim (value/min/max/low/high/optimum + valuetext), always determinate, with three derivable semantic value-zones. Distinct by ARIA design from `progress`/`role=progressbar` (task completion over time) and `status-indicator` (discrete lifecycle state) — folding a continuous scalar into either mis-types it (the Chakra `role=progressbar` defect). A gauge is a radial `presentation`, not a separate intent. Ratified #1410 (Fork 1a); WE owns the intent, FUI the rendered block (constellation-placement).

presentation.description: How the bounded scalar is drawn. `bar` is the native-first default (a horizontal track, the native `<meter>` shape); `radial` renders the same contract as an arc/gauge (a presentation, never a separate intent — rating and password-strength are likewise meter presentations, not standards).

zones.description: Whether the semantic value-zones derived from `low`/`high`/`optimum` are shown (drives colour: below-low / between / above-high, relative to where `optimum` sits). `none` = a plain magnitude bar; `low-high-optimum` = the three-zone semantic meter (e.g. disk usage greening toward empty, reddening toward full).

description:
What this is

A bounded-scalar readout: a static quantitative value within a known range — disk usage, a score, remaining capacity, password strength. It adopts the native <meter> / role=meter contract verbatim: value, min (default 0), max (default 1), low, high, optimum, and valuetext (a human-readable form of the value, e.g. "3 of 5 stars"). Realizing build for the ratified #1410 placement call.

Not progress, not status

ARIA defines meter and progressbar as two separate roles by design ("the meter should not be used to indicate progress … use the progressbar role instead"). A meter is a position in [min,max], always determinate, with zones; progress is task completion over time (may be indeterminate, no zones); status-indicator is a discrete lifecycle enum. Three value-types, three homes — forcing a continuous scalar through the others mis-types it.

Zones

The semantic colour comes from where value falls relative to low/high and which side optimum sits on — the native <meter>'s three-zone model. zones: none draws a plain magnitude bar; zones: low-high-optimum draws the semantic meter (the colour is the theme's, derived from the zone, never an author hex).

Presentation

bar (default) is the native horizontal track. radial renders the identical contract as an arc/gauge — a presentation, not a new intent (per readout-placement-by-value-type). The accessible role=meter + aria-valuenow/valuetext are identical across presentations.

Composition

Composes Web Theme (the zone colours resolve from tokens) and the native-first baseline (adopt <meter> verbatim). WE owns this intent contract; FUI ships the rendered block (#1468).
